in-re-thomas-grant-neusom-2025-05-08.lesson: One-year federal suspension follows when attorneys hedge on AI use and ignore court orders to produce cited cases.

matter-of-raven-investigations-security-.lesson: Even GAO protests now flag AI-pattern citations; unrepresented protesters face future bar referrals if counsel enters.

harris-v-take-two-interactive-software-2.lesson: Pro-se Rule 11 warnings are multiplying; treat them as early-warning indicators for your pro-se opposing parties.

rotonde-v-stewart-title-insurance-co-202.lesson: New York trial courts now cite Dowlah and Samuel as the baseline warning for fabricated AI citations.

x-v-board-of-trustees-of-governors-state.lesson: Rule 11 applies fully to pro-se litigants; a single unlocatable citation is enough to trigger an on-record warning.

lacey-v-state-farm-general-insurance-202.lesson: Paid tools like CoCounsel and Westlaw AI are not a defense; require a second-attorney verification pass before any AI-assisted brief is filed.

flowz-digital-v-caroline-dalal-2025-05-0.lesson: LexisNexis marketing 'hallucination-free' does not shift the verification duty; counsel still owns every cite.

wilt-v-department-of-the-navy-2025-05-02.lesson: Courts now openly label plausible-but-nonexistent FTCA and Title VII cites as AI-generated on the face of the pleading.

gustafson-v-amazoncom-2025-04-30.lesson: Exhibit misrepresentations paired with fake citations increase Rule 11 exposure even for pro-se litigants.

moales-v-land-rover-cherry-hill-2025-04-.lesson: District of Connecticut treats pervasive AI-style misstatements of law as Rule 11 risk, not just jurisdictional failure.

benjamin-v-costco-wholesale-corp-2025-04.lesson: Consumer AI tools under time pressure produce five fake cases; ban non-approved tools and require verification logs for sworn filings.

nichols-v-walmart-2025-04-23.lesson: Eleventh Circuit affirmance of Rule 11 dismissal for fake AI cites signals appellate appetite is hardening.

brown-v-patel-et-al-2025-04-22.lesson: Misrepresenting what a real case stands for is treated alongside fabrications when the pattern looks AI-generated.

ferris-v-amazoncom-services-2025-04-16.lesson: Seven ChatGPT-fabricated cites cost a pro-se plaintiff a cost-shifting sanction; courts are monetizing AI misconduct.

crystal-truong-et-al-v-flint-hills-resou.lesson: Non-suiting the case dodges a show-cause ruling but the CLE commitment still goes on your file; act before the OSC issues.

bischoff-v-south-carolina-department-of-.lesson: State administrative courts are now invoking frivolous-proceedings statutes against AI-fabricated cites.

bevins-v-colgate-palmolive-co-2025-04-10.lesson: Ignoring a standing AI order plus refusing to explain cites gets your appearance struck and your bar notified; always comply and disclose.
